Title: Erratum: "The GALFA-HI Compact Cloud Catalog" (2012, ApJ, 758, 44) 
Authors: Saul D.R., Peek J.E.G., Grcevich J., Putman M.E., Douglas K.A., 
         Korpela E.J., Stanimirovic S., Heiles C., Gibson S.J., Lee M., 
         Begum A., Brown A.R.H., Burkhart B., Hamden E.T., Pingel N.M., 
         Tonnesen S.
Table: DR1 GALFA-HI Cloud Catalog
================================================================================
Byte-by-byte Description of file: apj517658t1_mrt.txt
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Bytes Format Units       Label     Explanations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1- 14 A14    ---         ID        Cloud identifier (1)
  16- 17 I2     h           RAh       Hour of Right Ascension (J2000) (2)
  19- 20 I2     min         RAm       Minute of Right Ascension (J2000) (2)
  22- 23 I2     s           RAs       Second of Right Ascension (J2000) (2)
  25- 26 I2     deg         DEd       Degree of Declination (J2000) (2)
  28- 29 I2     arcmin      DEm       Arcminute of Declination (J2000) (2)
  31- 36 F6.2   deg         GLON      Galactic longitude
  38- 43 F6.2   deg         GLAT      Galactic latitude
  45- 49 F5.1   ---         sigmaMax  Maximum S/N of cloud (3)
  51- 54 F4.1   arcmin      Size      Angular FWHM of cloud (4) 
  56- 62 F7.2   km/s        VLSR      Local Standard of Rest velocity (5)
  64- 70 F7.2   km/s        VGSR      Galactic Standard of Rest velocity (6)
  72- 76 F5.2   km/s        DelV      FWHM in velocity (7) 
  78- 82 F5.2   K           TPkb      Peak brightness temperature (8)
  84- 86 I3     10+18/cm2   NHI       Maximum HI column density (9)
      88 A1     ---       f_NHI       [*] Flag on NHI (10)
  90- 95 F6.3   Jy.km/s     Stot      Total flux of cloud (11)
  97-101 A5     ---         Pop       Cloud population classification (12)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Note (1): Clouds are named by the right ascension (in degrees), declination, 
          and VLSR velocity of the peak in the convolved cube.
Note (2): Of the peak in the convolved cube. Note that this is not necessarily 
          the location of peak flux, or the spatial center of the region of 
          interest (see Section 3.1).
Note (3): In the convolved cube. This is a measure of the significance 
          of the cloud. 
Note (4): Assuming a circular cloud, 2(Area/{pi})^0.5^, where the area is that 
          of the half maximum value contour shown in the catalog images 
          (Figure 5). Due to our size constraint of 20' along any axis, the 
          selection function for elongated clouds drops below 20'.
Note (5): Of the peak of the fit to the average spectrum.
Note (6): Defined by VGSR=VLSR+220sin(GLON)cos(GLAT).
Note (7): Of the fit to the average spectrum.
Note (8): After Galactic subtraction.
Note (9): Calculated using the fit of the spectrum through the peak of the 
          cloud in the unconvolved data.
Note (10): 
    * = the fitting failed on the maximum spectrum thus the average column 
        density is reported as a lower limit.
Note (11): Determined by summing over the ROI in the Galactic background 
           subtracted data.
Note (12): Using the scheme detailed in Section 4:
    HVC = (high velocity cloud) |VLSR| > 90 km/s and near a known HVC complex;
     GC = (galaxy candidate) |VLSR| > 90 km/s and far from known HVC complex;
   CLVC = (cold low-velocity cloud) |VLSR| < 90 km/s and DelV < 15 km/s;
   WLVC = (warm low-velocity cloud) |VLSR| < 90 km/s, DelV > 15 km/s, 
          and not Q3;
     Q3 = (warm, positive low-velocity cloud in the third Galactic quadrant) 
          90 > VLSR > 0 km/s, DelV > 15 km/s, and 270 > GLON > 180.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
